title: edge-console component configuration
description:
The shape of `component.global` for edge-console. The canonical EdgeCommons config schema leaves `component.global` open, so this document is what validates the console's own configuration — in the component's tests, in `edgecommons component validate`, and in the deployment compatibility guard. edge-console runs one console per node and reads no `component.instances[]`, so this document declares no `#/$defs/instance`.

Every key is optional: the gateway falls back to a built-in default for anything absent or out of range (`ConsoleConfig::default`, `gateway/src/config.rs`). What this schema catches is a key that is misspelled, misplaced, or of the wrong type — which the gateway would otherwise silently ignore.

console.description: All console configuration. The gateway reads nothing under `component.global` outside this object.

ws.description: The HTTPS/WebSocket listener the browser connects to.

bindAddress.description: Secure by default: loopback only. Container and Kubernetes deployments must set `0.0.0.0` explicitly, because loopback is unreachable through a published port.

webRoot.description: Directory served as the console UI. Absent means the UI bundled with the gateway.

allowedOrigins.description: Browser origins permitted to connect. Empty means same-origin only.

apps.description: Additional single-purpose UIs served alongside the console, each scoped to the roles and capabilities it is granted.

staleness.description: How long after a component's expected heartbeat interval the console calls it late, stale, then offline. Multipliers apply to each component's own interval, so a 1 s heartbeat and a 60 s heartbeat are judged on their own terms.

defaultIntervalSecs.description: Assumed heartbeat interval for a component that has not declared one.

events.description: Bounds on the in-memory event ring. The console holds no database; these caps are what keep a chatty fleet from growing the gateway without limit.

maxSeriesPoints.description: Points retained per series — the width of the live chart window.

defaultTail.description: Records returned when a tail request names no count.

runtime.description: Process-level tuning for the gateway itself.

mallocArenaMax.description: Caps glibc's per-thread arenas, which otherwise inflate resident memory on a many-core edge box.

rbac.description: Which roles may issue which command verbs.

defaultRole.description: The role assigned to a session that presents none. It must be one of `roles`.

roles.description: Role name to its verb policy.

commands.description: Timeouts applied to commands the console issues onto the bus.

maxTimeoutMs.description: Upper bound for any single command. Clamped to the bridge's reply TTL (60000 ms) — a longer timeout could not be honoured, because the reply would already have expired.

clock.description: Detection of host clock steps, which would otherwise show up as impossible timestamps across the fleet.

stepAlarmThresholdMs.description: NTP slews stay under 128 ms, so a backward step at or beyond this is unambiguous clock trouble rather than normal correction.

app.description: One registered application. An entry the gateway cannot make sense of is skipped with a warning rather than failing startup, so a typo here costs you the app silently — which is the reason to validate it here instead.

id.description: Lower-kebab, starting with a letter and ending alphanumeric. Unique across `apps`.

allowedRoles.description: Roles permitted to open the app. Empty means every role.

capabilities.description: The console data the app may read. Anything not listed is not served to it.

rolePolicy.description: Verb lists for one role. `deny` is evaluated after `allow`, so an explicit denial always wins.

allow.description: Command verbs this role may issue. `*` permits all.
